page.title: Sailing & Naval
page.description: In Quinfall the sea is a separate game: 4 ship classes × 2 factions, 7 harbours, 3 fishing categories and a Kraken that descends 3 times a day. Naval combat lives only on the PvP channel; reward and risk balance each other.

hero.title: 7 harbours, 4 ship classes, 3 Krakens/day.
hero.body: Four hulls from raft to warship, each built in either Trader or Pirate role. Cannons fit only Frigates and Warships (since the June 26, 2025 patch). At a harbour the Harbor Master runs your fleet; sea caravans buy the package, the Kraken falls only to a naval crew. Switching channels means calling the ship to a harbour first.

fleet.title: 4 Hulls × 2 Factions
fleet.subtitle: The same four classes, two purposes. Trader and Pirate ships are identical to build; their roles split on the PvP channel — one carries the package, the other hunts it.

cannonRule.title: Cannon Rule (June 26, 2025 patch)
cannonRule.body: Rafts and Boats can no longer mount cannons. Only Frigates and Warships are armable. Small hulls are now hauling-only — keep that in mind before you pick a fight.

trader.title: Trader Ships
trader.body: Carry packages on the PvP channel. Goal: pocket the gold gap between harbours. Cargo and hull integrity are everything.

pirate.title: Pirate Ships
pirate.body: Same four classes in pirate role. Used to attack Trader ships and loot their packages. Build cost identical to the Trader version.

raftTrader.name: Raft · Trader
raftTrader.body: Starter raft. Coastal packages and inshore fishing. No cannons.

boatTrader.name: Boat · Trader
boatTrader.body: Bigger cargo, still unarmed. The first real long-haul transport.

frigateTrader.name: Frigate · Trader
frigateTrader.body: First ship that can mount cannons. Carries valuable packages with escort.

warshipTrader.name: Warship · Trader
warshipTrader.body: The biggest trader. Large crew, heavy cannons, guild scale.

raftPirate.name: Raft · Pirate
raftPirate.body: More about a quick getaway than raiding. No cannons; if you get chased, it's over.

boatPirate.name: Boat · Pirate
boatPirate.body: Pirating 101. Stack a few crew and chase down small Trader rafts.

frigatePirate.name: Frigate · Pirate
frigatePirate.body: Cannon-armed pirate hull. The main predator on the PvP channel.

warshipPirate.name: Warship · Pirate
warshipPirate.body: The biggest pirate ship. Guild ops and major Trader raids.

classTable.title: Build Costs
classTable.note: Coin cost is identical for both factions. Materials add up: lower tiers use cheap wood / iron, top tiers want high-quality Pine Araucaria + Sycamore + MQ Iron.

premium.title: Cosmetic Fleet
premium.subtitle: Four special ships present in the game data. Likely unlocked through the shop and events; the unlock channel isn't fully verified yet from primary sources.

mermaidShip.name: Mermaid Ship
mermaidShip.body: Mermaid-themed hull, blue-green palette cosmetic ship.

charonsFerryShip.name: Charon's Ferry Ship
charonsFerryShip.body: Underworld-ferry aesthetic — dark purple / bone-textured ship.

medusaShip.name: Medusa Ship
medusaShip.body: Serpent motif on the hull, greenish tones, mythic theme.

tridentShip.name: Trident Ship
tridentShip.body: Trident / Poseidon theme, bright gold accents.

note.title: Data source note
note.body: These four ships exist in the client items.json; community wikis and Steam patch notes don't list a sales channel yet. The V-Coin shop is the most likely source — to be confirmed when surfaced in-game.

harbors.title: Harbor Master and Services
harbors.subtitle: Seven major harbours on the world map. Every one hosts the same Harbor Master NPC: ship management, ship trading, caravan packages, ship building, towing.

edgeshore.body: Main exit harbour on the east coast. Wide variety of fish; ideal for starter trader routes.

silvercrest.body: A harbour built around silver mining. Heavy ore traffic and trade.

maplewood.body: Forest meets coast. Cheap heartwood / stormoak market for shipbuilders; not a high-payout harbour.

thessahazy.body: Misty, mystical harbour. Higher rare-drop rates but easy to lose your way; experienced captains only.

puttonas.body: Southern warm-water harbour. Tropical fish, corals; raw material source for cooking recipes.

ellingliers.body: Northwestern ice sea. Cold-water fish only here; cold-resist gear required.

traystable.body: The central hub of the map. The highest caravan-pack payouts depart from here.

harbors.servicesSubtitle: Six core services through one NPC — the harbour's command desk.

shipsInHarbor.title: My Ships in Harbor
shipsInHarbor.body: Lists every ship docked here; summon and repair from this menu.

shipTrading.title: Ship Trading
shipTrading.body: Official channel for player-to-player ship sales / transfers.

barter.title: Barter
barter.body: Goods-for-goods exchange: trade package contents for other items.

caravanPackages.title: Caravan Packages
caravanPackages.body: The harbour's packages live here. Payout depends on this departure harbour.

shipBuilding.title: Ship Building
shipBuilding.body: Start a new ship build through the Harbor Master.

towing.title: Towing
towing.body: Tow a stuck ship back to the harbour for a fee.

payoutTable.label: PAYOUT TABLE
payoutTable.title: Estimated Payout per Harbor
payoutTable.note: From community observation, by departure harbour. Traystable is the highest (~1,500g); Edgeshore and Silvercrest start at the floor (~500g).

combat.title: Cannons, Channels, Piracy
combat.subtitle: Naval combat only runs on the PvP channel. The PvE channel is pure transport; no attacks, no losses. Items below are confirmed against official patch notes and the Companion sailing guide.

pvpOnly.title: PvP channel only
pvpOnly.body: Ship combat is only active on PvP channels. On PvE the ship is a transport — other players can't attack.

mannedCannons.title: Manned or automatic cannons
mannedCannons.body: Cannons are loaded and fired by crew; some can be set to automatic. Voice coordination is a serious advantage.

durability.title: Ships don't decay
durability.body: Companion: 'Ships do not rot or decay over time; you only lose durability from combat damage and collisions.' Time-away costs nothing.

channelSwitch.title: Channel swap needs harbour
channelSwitch.body: No mid-ocean channel swap. Call the ship to a harbour first; if you dock, on-board caravan packages are destroyed.

piracy.title: Piracy and boarding
piracy.body: On a PvP channel, pirate ships hunt traders and loot packages. Side-by-side boarding (sending crew across) is the classic move.

cannonRestriction.title: Raft/Boat cannon ban
cannonRestriction.body: Rafts and Boats can no longer mount cannons. All naval firepower is concentrated in Frigates and Warships.

kraken.title: Open-Sea Naval Boss
kraken.subtitle: Quinfall's most iconic world boss. Ship-only — can't be fought from land. Descends three times a day, far more often than any other world boss.

spotlight.title: Kraken
spotlight.body: Surfaces from the deep and shreds hulls with its tentacles. The Cursed Realms patch added a melee pattern and bumped damage by 50%. A guild-scale naval crew + Frigate/Warship is mandatory.

phases.title: Attack Patterns
phases.note: Some phase names come from community observation; the official mechanic text isn't out yet.

surface.title: Surface emerge
surface.body: Kraken breaches; every cannon can line up. The opening window is the densest damage opportunity.

tentacle.title: Tentacle Slam
tentacle.body: Targets one ship in a single heavy strike. Spread positions so it can't sequence-hit the same hull.

inkCloud.title: Ink Cloud
inkCloud.body: A dark ink cloud cuts vision and accuracy; a brief retreat window.

whirlpool.title: Whirlpool
whirlpool.body: Pulls the area to centre. Open the sail, push the engine, get out — or you ram straight into it.

underwater.title: Underwater Phase
underwater.body: Dives for a while. Predict the resurface point; keep harpoons ready.

rewards.title: Drop Pool
rewards.note: Kraken-specific rewards are gathered from community sources; some (e.g. the Sea Champion title) aren't fully verified in primary sources.

materials.title: Kraken parts
materials.body: Top-tier crafting materials — the input for naval set and Kraken-line weapons.

gold.title: Heavy gold
gold.body: Total gold per run is significant; a guild-economy weekly stream.

navalSet.title: Naval set pieces
navalSet.body: Crew-bonus naval set pieces drop in the same pool.

title.title: Sea Champion title
title.body: A permanent title — discussed in the community; the exact in-game label isn't confirmed yet.

fishing.title: 3 Categories, 2 Modes, 1 Token Economy
fishing.subtitle: Fishing is one of the verified economy lanes: three categories (freshwater, saltwater, deep sea); active (E) or AFK (T) mode; output converts at the Armorer into enhancement materials.

categories.title: Fishing Categories
categories.body: Quinfall fishing is officially split into three categories. The harbour-flavoured zones the community talks about (warm/cold/misty) are flavour on top of this, not the core system.

freshwater.name: Freshwater
freshwater.body: Inland water sources; easiest access and the largest fish supply.

saltwater.name: Saltwater
saltwater.body: Sea water near the harbour. The main fishing ground for coastal fish.

deepSea.name: Deep Sea
deepSea.body: Far from harbour, ship required. Larger and rarer fish; legendary species near the Kraken zone.

activeMode.title: Active mode (E)
activeMode.body: Active casting with the E key. Reaction-based, higher chance of quality fish.

afkMode.title: AFK mode (T)
afkMode.body: Automatic fishing on the T key. Slow but hands-free; great for long sessions.

tokens.title: Profession tokens
tokens.body: Sell your catch to the Fisherman NPC and you earn profession tokens.

enhancement.title: Armorer exchange
enhancement.body: Trade tokens at the Armorer NPC for enhancement materials (T → Exchange).

tokenEconomy.title: Token → Enhancement Economy
tokenEconomy.body: Steam community guide: 'Trade your gathered materials for profession tokens... go to an Armorer NPC, press T, Exchange, and trade your tokens for enhancement materials.'

strategy.title: Which Captain for Which Path?
strategy.subtitle: Safe shores for new captains, gold lanes for traders, PvP hunting for pirates, guild-scale Kraken runs.

starter.title: 🌱 New Captain
bullets: First ship: a raft — 60,000 coin entry, fine for inshore fishing + short routes.
Start on the PvE channel with fishing and token farming; sell to the Armorer for enhancement mats.
Before stepping up to Boat / Frigate, learn the wood economy at Maplewood.
Channel switching only at a harbour — docking destroys the package, mind the order.

trader.title: 💰 Trader
bullets: Frigate Trader — first armable hull, run valuable packages with escort.
Deliver the package before docking elsewhere — docking destroys it.
On a high-value run bring a Frigate / Warship escort; don't be free pirate food.

pirate.title: 🏴‍☠ Pirate
bullets: Start with Frigate Pirate; Warship Pirate is a guild investment.
Camp the trade lanes on PvP channel; chase lone Trader Boats / Frigates.
Keep 4-6 melee crew for boarding actions.
Strike before the trader hits the harbour; once it docks the package is gone.

kraken.title: 🐙 Kraken Run
bullets: Guild coordination is mandatory; one ship can't drop it. Mix Frigates / Warships, at least 3 ships.
Crew: 1 captain + 4-8 gunners + 2 support. Don't even try without voice.
Spread on Whirlpool, keep harpoons ready after the Underwater phase.
The drop pool is the main source of naval set + crafting materials — schedule it weekly.
